user_query:
I'm building a meditation app and need a small event-triggered ingestor that pulls calming ambient audio from public-domain sources to seed our soundscape library. Please implement a script or small tool that treats changes to input/ambience_events.json as the trigger and does the following:

1) Inspect the local workspace to detect which events in input/ambience_events.json are new or changed (compare against a persistent state file at state/processed_events.json). An event is "new/changed" if it is missing from state/processed_events.json or if the hash of its serialized configuration differs from the stored hash.

2) For each new/changed event, find and download up to max_downloads audio files that match the event keywords and are clearly licensed as public domain or CC0. Use official item pages from a stable, reputable source such as the Internet Archive (archive.org) that clearly displays license metadata on the item page. Do not require credentials, private APIs, or logins. Only select items whose license string matches one of the values listed in input/allowed_licenses.json. Skip items without an explicit allowed license.

3) Extract structured metadata from the source item web pages for each downloaded file and save it in a per-event manifest. At minimum record: source_domain, item_identifier (if applicable), page_url, file_url, file_name, file_size_bytes, file_sha256, title, creator, license, and duration_seconds (if available from the page). If you skip candidates, include a brief reason in the per-event manifest under skipped_reasons.

4) Save outputs as:
   - output/events/<event_id>/downloads/… (the downloaded audio files; prefer WAV or MP3 and limit each file to <= 20 MB)
   - output/events/<event_id>/manifest.json (see fields above plus event_id, keywords, processed_at ISO timestamp)
   - output/combined_manifest.json (a flat array of all items across all processed events, each with an added event_id field)
   - state/processed_events.json (map of event_id to a record with config_hash, processed_at, and count of downloaded items) used to avoid reprocessing unchanged events

5) The tool must:
   - Inspect files and directories to determine which events need processing and to avoid re-downloading existing files (verify by file_sha256 when possible)
   - Download remote audio resources from the Internet only from official pages that expose license metadata (e.g., archive.org item pages)
   - Extract structured data from both the local input JSON files and the source web pages (HTML) to populate the manifests

Inputs you must use:
- input/ambience_events.json (defines events, keywords, and per-event limits)
- input/allowed_licenses.json (whitelist for acceptable license strings)

Outputs must be reproducible locally: if no suitable items are found for an event, still produce output/events/<event_id>/manifest.json with items: [] and a skipped_reasons array explaining why. Please keep the implementation self-contained (no cloud services or credentials) and runnable in a normal local workspace. Avoid hardcoding specific URLs; rely on item pages from the official source that match the keywords and license constraints.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
